round1: Round 1 is done, and it seems most of you are better at watching hockey than predicting it. Derrick, Jamie, and Nickall, your brackets are already dust; meanwhile, Steph nailed the Habs sweep, and a couple of you actually saw the Kings' comeback coming.
round2: Round 2 is done, and it seems most of you were watching reruns. While everyone got Chicago right, your brackets are now officially compost after a collective face-plant on Boston and Pittsburgh. Jamie and Ryan, congrats on actually seeing the Canadiens, and Nickall, you were the only one to avoid picking a single wrong team, a low bar for success.
round3: Round 3 is over, and it seems most of you need a new crystal ball after overwhelmingly backing the wrong horse in the East. Nathan, however, is now accepting applications for his new psychic hotline after perfectly calling both series, including LAK in 7 and NYR in 6. The rest of you are just here to make him look good.
round4: Round 4 is in the books, and the Kings made quick work of the Rangers in 5. Ryan and Steph are the only ones who actually watched the series, nailing LAK in 5, while the rest of you clearly missed the memo. For those who picked the Rangers, perhaps a new hobby is in order.
overall: Alright, the 2014 NHL playoffs are finally over, with the Kings somehow grinding out a Stanley Cup victory, and in our pool, Nathan absolutely ran away with it, racking up a commanding 55 points to prove he actually pays attention to hockey. Ryan put up a respectable, if ultimately futile, fight finishing a single point behind, while Stephanie and Jaclyn managed to avoid complete embarrassment in the middle of the pack. As for Nickall, a truly pathetic 25 points with eight teams picked means they've secured the coveted "pool loser" title, proving that sometimes, less *is* more, especially when your picks are universally terrible.
